reviewTitle: I had a very disappointing experience…
reviewDescription: I had a very disappointing experience with this trip overall. I booked a regular SUV nearly a month in advance, only for it to be cancelled just a few hours before my trip, forcing me to scramble for alternatives through customer support at the last minute. I was given a luxury vehicle as a replacement, which significantly increased my expenses due to premium gas requirements and, later, a very costly tire replacement after a sidewall gash during the trip. To make matters worse, the car was not properly cleaned inside or outside at pickup. The experience with customer support was equally frustrating, as the representative I spoke with was rude, dismissive, and showed very little accountability or empathy for the situation. What should have been a smooth and budgeted trip became unnecessarily stressful and expensive due to circumstances completely outside of my control, and I expected a far better level of professionalism and customer care from Turo.

reviewTitle: Paid for Enhanced Roadside Assistance and Received Chaos
reviewDescription:
I rarely leave negative reviews, but my recent experience with Turo was honestly one of the worst customer service experiences I’ve had in a very long time.

I rented a vehicle and paid extra for Enhanced Roadside Assistance specifically for situations like this. During my trip, the front passenger tire suddenly went flat after a short 20-minute drive. I parked the car briefly, came back, and the tire was completely deflated. There was no damage to the rim, no visible puncture, no nail, and even the roadside mechanic later said the tire appeared repairable.

What should have been a straightforward roadside assistance situation turned into an exhausting 18+ hour nightmare filled with misinformation, contradictions, broken promises, and extremely poor communication.

I spent HOURS on the phone with multiple agents. One representative told me I could return the vehicle early and receive a refund or price match for another vehicle despite my reservation being non-refundable. Another representative confirmed the same thing. Later, the “Experience Team” completely contradicted them and told me I would receive no refund at all because my reservation was non-refundable.

Not only was I repeatedly given false information, but every time I spoke to a new person, I had to explain the entire situation all over again because no proper notes were being added to my case.

Roadside assistance itself was completely disorganized:

I was promised a tow truck, but instead a mechanic arrived in a personal vehicle.
The spare tire was low on air, and I was told inflating it required an entirely separate authorization request.
I had to call back AGAIN late at night and wait on hold just to authorize air for the spare tire.
One representative was so confused she thought I was either the host or the roadside provider instead of the guest.
I was promised a tow for 9:00 AM the next morning. Nobody showed up.
After waiting around all morning, I finally called the towing company myself and was told delays were happening because of Eid al-Adha, but nobody from Turo or the towing company had proactively informed me.
Later, I was told the tow would not happen until around 7:00 PM.
Then I found out the vehicle wouldn’t even be brought directly to the host, who was actually willing to repair the car that same day.

Meanwhile, I was still actively paying for a vehicle I could not use.

The most frustrating part is that the host was actually helpful and cooperative throughout this entire situation. The problem was entirely the lack of coordination and professionalism from Turo support and their roadside partners.

If this is what “Enhanced Roadside Assistance” looks like, then it is incredibly disappointing and unprofessional. The amount of time, stress, confusion, and inconvenience this caused was unacceptable.

This experience has seriously damaged my trust in Turo, and I will definitely think twice before ever using the platform again.

I booked it three months in advance to secure a good price since long-term rentals are usually difficult. Five days before my trip, my reservation was canceled. As expected, I couldn't find any car within the original price range, and I had to pay 90% more for the second booking. I contacted customer service to ask if they could offer a coupon, discount, or credit for the higher cost, but they simply replied that there is nothing they can do. I learned that, unlike with insurance companies, where prices are locked in and cars are always available, I should never rent a long-term drive on Turo again. Also their customer service is awful. they are not helpful at all.

My experience started off as a 5+ rating, but unfortunately it quickly went sour. At around 5:30 a.m., in a dark airport parking lot, I accidentally parked in the wrong spot. Shortly after, I received a rude and nasty text from the host questioning why I hadn’t followed their parking instructions.
I tried to explain that while they provided instructions, the app itself guided me to a different area, which caused the confusion. Instead of understanding, the host responded in a very condescending manner, saying that if I had “read better,” the situation could have been avoided. They also informed me that I would be responsible for any improper parking fees—which I had no issue accepting, since I acknowledged the mistake.
What bothered me was the tone and attitude. There was no reason to be so rude or dismissive. As someone who works in the hospitality industry, I understand the importance of customer service, and this interaction fell far below acceptable standards.
I will not be using this host again. The conversation is documented in the app’s messaging system, and I would hope it’s reviewed, because this kind of behavior shouldn’t go unaddressed.

reviewTitle: Une expérience de commande extrêmement mauvaise
reviewDescription:
Le 2 avril, j'ai réservé un pick-up Renault Master Extended Luxury de 2014 (numéro de réservation : 55770291) via la plateforme Turo. J'ai fourni tous les documents nécessaires avant de confirmer la réservation.

La plateforme a accepté ces documents, confirmé la réservation et traité le paiement de 1 117,77 €.

Cependant, environ 10 heures avant l'heure de prise en charge prévue, Turo a annulé unilatéralement la réservation, prétextant un lien avec la date de délivrance de mon permis de conduire.

À ce jour :

- Je n'ai jamais reçu le véhicule ;

- Je n'ai bénéficié d'aucun service ;

- Malgré l'envoi de nombreux courriels et messages de relance au service client, je n'ai reçu aucune réponse depuis plus d'un mois.

Étant donné que la plateforme a accepté la réservation et encaissé le paiement intégral, puis a annulé le service à la dernière minute sans proposer de remboursement raisonnable, j'estime qu'il s'agit d'un abus et d'une violation de mes droits de consommateur.

Je demande une enquête sur cette affaire et un remboursement équitable.

I had an extremely frustrating experience with Turo while trying to rent a vehicle for my anniversary. I attempted to rent a vehicle through Turo and selected Affirm as my payment option. Affirm showed that I had a scheduled payment, but Turo showed that I had no upcoming trip.

I contacted Turo support and was told there was no trip planned under my account. The problem is that no one could give me any confirmation number, cancellation number, or clear documentation because they said there was no trip. Meanwhile, Affirm still showed a payment connected to Turo. That left me stuck between Turo and Affirm with no clear answer and no rental.

After that, I still could not rent from Turo at all. I tried using a different card, a different phone number, and even a private browser window, but I still could not complete a booking. Turo could not clearly explain whether my account was restricted, whether there was a verification issue, whether it was connected to the Affirm problem, or what steps I needed to take to fix it.

This was not a casual rental. This was for a special anniversary trip, and the lack of clear communication completely ruined the experience. Turo should not allow a customer to end up with a payment issue through Affirm while showing no actual trip, then fail to explain why the customer can no longer rent.

Very disappointing customer service, poor communication, and no real resolution. I would be very cautious using Turo, especially if you are renting for an important event or using Affirm as a payment option.

reviewTitle: I have not rented a car in the last 30…
reviewDescription: I have not rented a car in the last 30 years  . . . HONESTLY!!! Because of my inexperience I rented a Mini Cooper for 25.5 hours costing $187+. What happened: the rental was from 10am until 10am the following day. The car to be picked up and returned to the short term lot at PIE airport, FL. Things got a little tight for the 10am return time. I texted Turo to ask if it was OK to return the car at 11:30am. The response was "that will be fine" and nothing further was advised. Knowing what I know now I honestly have no issue with your rental policies. My issue is that at the time of the request I should have been advised that to gain 1.5 hours I would be charged another day's rental, $93.51. Had I realized the 24 hour policy, I would certainly found a way to return the car by 10am. That is why I am upset with the way you folks do business and will be reluctant to use your company in the future.

reviewDescription: The trip was a complete success. The pick up was easy and the car was clean. The car was a hybrid and gas was reasonable. Bernie was a great host!

Dear Turo Executive Support and Customer Experience teams,

I’m requesting an executive review regarding Reservation #57151139 due to what appears to be a contradictory platform workflow and misleading operational guidance that resulted in an improper return charge.

I’ve already gone through standard support channels and received a denial. However, I do not believe the substance of the issue was fully evaluated.

My concern is not primarily the dollar amount. My concern is that the Turo app workflow, host instructions, and operational reality conflicted in a way that created a predictable guest failure scenario.

Summary of concerns:

1. Misleading airport pickup representation BY HOST

The listing and booking flow represented the pickup as Philadelphia International Airport (attached). However, the actual pickup process required transportation to a third-party off-airport parking facility (“The Parking Spot North”), including shuttle logistics, additional wait time, and a non-standard pickup/exit process that was materially different from a typical airport pickup experience that are NOT DISCLOSED prior to airport arrival and vehicle check-in.

As someone who has rented through Turo multiple times in multiple cities, including other airport-adjacent Turo experiences, this setup was significantly less transparent than other airport listings I’ve used (e.g. San Diego Airport utilizing near-airport locations)

2. Contradictory return instructions

The host later messaged that returning the vehicle anywhere other than Parking Spot North would result in fees.

However, during the actual Turo app return workflow, the app presented multiple approved return locations, including airport parking garages. I selected Garage D through the Turo app workflow, and the app allowed me to continue through checkout without warning that this selection would trigger an “improper return” fee.

I reasonably relied on the  platform workflow presented by Turo during checkout.

If the app permitted a prohibited location selection without warning, I do not believe the resulting fee should be imposed on the guest.

3. Inability to recreate app evidence after trip completion

Support denied my appeal largely because I could not provide screenshots of the checkout workflow. Unfortunately, once the trip was completed, I could no longer access or recreate that exact workflow inside the Turo app.

I would ask Turo to independently review:
- the guest checkout workflow presented for this reservation
- the return-location selection options shown during checkout
- whether Garage D was presented as an approved/selectable option
- whether any warning was shown that selecting Garage D would still trigger an improper-return fee

Requested resolution:
- reversal of the improper-return related charges
- internal review of the contradictory guest workflow
- review of whether this host’s airport representation and operational flow align with Turo marketplace standards

I’ve been a repeat Turo customer and would strongly prefer to resolve this professionally and directly with Turo rather than escalate externally.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

reviewTitle: I am filing this complaint due to…
reviewDescription:
I am filing this complaint due to Turo’s failure to resolve a dispute regarding a $50 promotional credit and their failure to uphold a commitment made by their support team.
On May 23, 2026, I canceled a car rental reservation for an upcoming August 2026 trip. Because this was canceled well in advance, there was no financial impact on the host or the platform. However, Turo failed to refund the $50 promotional credit applied to this booking.
When I contacted support, I was informed that the credit was forfeited and that I would essentially have to wait until next year to receive another one—a highly unreasonable response for a loyal customer of five years. I was then given escalation case number 20260523-8Q0TGW and was explicitly promised that a representative would contact me within 24 hours to discuss issuing the $50 as a travel credit.
As of May 25, 2026, no one has reached out to me, and the issue remains unresolved. I am seeking the immediate reinstatement of my $50 promotional credit. I believe that ignoring a customer's escalation case is poor business practice and violates the commitment to service that a company of Turo's size should uphold.

reviewTitle: I had what initially seemed like a…
reviewDescription:
I had what initially seemed like a great experience using Turo—until a dispute arose.

I rented a vehicle from May 18–21, but returned it early on May 20 at 9:09 PM. I parked the vehicle in the same back parking area where I originally picked it up, left the visitor parking pass displayed exactly as it was when I received the vehicle, refilled the gas tank, secured the car, and immediately notified the host through the Turo app. I also provided timestamped photos showing exactly where the vehicle was parked.

The host read my message, thanked me, and even asked me to leave a 5-star review. At no point did they mention any issue with the parking location or ask me to move the vehicle.

The next day, I was hit with an “improper return” charge, including fees and admin costs.

I appealed with Turo, providing screenshots of the in-app messages, acknowledgment from the host, and photos documenting the return. Despite this evidence, Turo denied the appeal.

My issue is less about the amount charged and more about the principle: if a host acknowledges receipt of a properly returned vehicle without objection, then later makes a contradictory claim, what meaningful protection does the guest actually have?

I wanted to like this platform because the convenience is great, but this experience left me with serious concerns about how disputes are evaluated and whether guest-provided evidence is given fair consideration

So, i'm new to the platform. This rental was my very first rental through Turo - I typically do the rental counter to be honest, and, if i'm able to get back in that position, I will continue using the rental counter. All was fine and well, I had a plan to use the Turo for gig work as my vehicle was reposessed 2 weeks ago. I get the car and there is ABSOLUTELY something wrong with it. I actually did message the owner and was like hey, i'm not going on any kind of road trip or anything, but is the car ok? It feels like it's having a really hard time shifting. Long story short, I was so uncomfortable driving the car that I did not even do what I was planning on doing with it. The whole week was a wash and I literally lost $200. When I realized what a horrible situation I was in, I, VERY reluctantly, requested an extension for one day so I could do one more gig route. It wasn't worth it. The cost of the extension plus the gas to do the gig work left me at a whopping $0. I could have done more gig work, but you can see through my rental that I literally used almost less than half of the miles allowed. It was a poor experience and i'm reluctant to try it out again. And yes, I gave the host and car 5 stars for everything, because i'm new, and it would have beens uper easy for the host to rate me poorly. I don'tk now that I will use Turo in the future, but, I don't want any kind of negative rating on my name.

All too many times especially with Speedie cars have interior damage or exterior damage, huge dents, & dings, & who wants to pay more than a rental ar company to rent a car that has damage outside? This is often not disclosed & should be immediately either a discount should be applied to the renter if you pull up & upon documentating the vehicle you see damages that wasn't made clear in the advertisement. 
I also think cars should be vacuumed out before presenting to a renter, not all cars have this isste but some. I'm paying often time more than I would at a rental counter, with limited miles of generally 200 miles a day vs unlimited with a rental company.

I reported the damage to the platform and provided the evidence on the same day, but it was only weeks later that they got back to me.

They made no effort at all to recover the costs from the other party’s insurance. In fact, it was only then that I realized their policy seems to be that the guest pays for the damage no matter who is responsible. This is the first time I have heard of such a ridiculous policy.

Their claims support staff were unprofessional, inefficient, and unfriendly. The whole process lasted half a year, and in the end, I paid the full amount of the excess. If this had been handled by a professional insurance company, I am sure the costs could have been covered by the other party’s insurance.

My advice is to use a professional rental company and don’t be tempted to use Turo. It was a total nightmare!!!

reviewTitle: Turo Denied Fuel Dispute Despite Extensive Evidence
reviewDescription:
As a first-time Turo user, this experience significantly changed my perception of the platform’s dispute process.

I returned the vehicle after refueling immediately before airport drop-off. When I parked and turned the vehicle off at return, the fuel gauge was showing full, and I messaged the host directly to let them know the vehicle had been returned with a full tank. I also followed all other checkout instructions and prompts provided through the Turo app.

I provided proof of the fuel purchase, Life360 mileage and location tracking showing I only drove approximately 8.5 miles after fueling, MPG calculations for the vehicle, and direct communication confirming the vehicle was returned full.

However, because I did not take a final dashboard fuel-gauge photo, Turo denied my dispute and stated I had no grounds for reimbursement reversal, despite all other supporting evidence provided.

What concerns me most is that the actual reimbursement amount did not reasonably match the amount of fuel potentially used before drop-off. I spent approximately $21 to refill the vehicle from around half a tank to full. Based on the vehicle’s MPG and the approximately 8.5 miles driven after fueling, the actual amount of fuel used before return would realistically have only been worth approximately $1 at most.

Instead, the host later refueled the vehicle approximately 33 miles away from the airport in a different state and submitted a refueling receipt for approximately $14, which I was then charged for along with additional convenience fees and taxes totaling over $27. The host’s own dashboard photo still showed the fuel gauge extremely close to full.

What also concerns me is that at no point during check-in or checkout was I specifically prompted or clearly warned that a timestamped dashboard fuel-gauge photo/video would effectively be the single most important piece of evidence in a fuel dispute. As a first-time user, I reasonably believed fuel receipts, mileage tracking, timestamps, and communication with the host would also be considered valid supporting documentation.

If Turo’s dispute process ultimately depends almost entirely on a final dashboard fuel photo, then the platform should explicitly require or strongly prompt renters to capture and upload these photos during checkout instead of dismissing all other evidence afterward.

My advice to future renters: over-document absolutely everything at return, including timestamped dashboard fuel and mileage photos/videos, even if you already have receipts and trip tracking data.

The important thing is, REVIEW receipts that arrive weeks or months after an agreement of charges. Dispute false charges with both the rip off company, it'screp and your CC bank.

Turo touts having transparency with costs, no hidden fees, etc.  I was charged an additional $45 delivery fee that was not on the initial total charges invoice.  I specifically shopped for car with airport delivery and no additional fee was stated.  I was warned about this habit of turo ahead of time.  I have my total charge invoice which is $45 lower.  Turo made a separate charge to my account of $45.  Since they have your card, they charge it.  Their policy states if you dispute charges with credit card company then they can't address it.  I sent email, no reply.  Go in app and get message "you can't go down this road" when looking to submit dispute.  Extremely disappointed and frustrated at being ripped off and don't seem to have means to resolve it with turo.  Hoping they respond to this review. Rented a vehicle through Turo using the app. The first issue was that the price was different on my phone vs my wife’s phone. That should have been my sign to use another vendor. But I went ahead with the rental and followed the prompts. I updated my driver’s license as prompted and declined the additional coverage since I have insurance already. The following day I received notification that my rental was canceled and I was being refunded minus an $80 fee. When I contacted Turo I was told repeatedly that I did not follow the pre check-in instructions including providing my drivers license. I sent PROOF that my drivers license was indeed updated in the app and only after that did the Agent admit that it was showing as updated. Suddenly, the issue turned into me not declining the additional coverage. I have proof that I declined that as well but after going back and forth with an Agent who herself seemed confused, I asked for the matter to be escalated. A few mins after ending the chat, I get an email saying I would not be refunded and the decision was final. It’s obvious the decision maker didn’t even take the time to fully investigate the issue. I’m frankly disgusted by the entire ordeal and will never use Turo again. Unless you plan on being frustrated to no end, I’d suggest rethinking their app and services.

Ryan’s Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe was absolutely outstanding and played a huge part in making our family trip to Leavenworth such a success.
The rental process was incredibly smooth from start to finish. Ryan was communicative, professional, and made pickup and drop-off effortless. The vehicle was exactly as described—clean, well-maintained, and in excellent condition.
The Grand Cherokee 4xe itself was the perfect vehicle for exploring the area. It was comfortable, spacious, and had plenty of room for our family and luggage. The hybrid system was a nice bonus, providing great fuel efficiency without sacrificing power or capability. Whether we were driving through town, heading to trailheads, or exploring the surrounding mountain roads, the Jeep handled everything with ease and confidence.
What really stood out was how worry-free the entire experience was. Having a reliable, comfortable vehicle allowed us to focus on enjoying our vacation, and Leavenworth was even more enjoyable because of it.
We wouldn’t hesitate to rent from Ryan again and would highly recommend both him and this Jeep to anyone visiting the area. Thanks for helping make an already great trip truly exceptional! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I returned the vehicle with a full tank of gas, yet the host still tried charging me an extra $10 for gas after already making over $500 from my trip, which felt extremely petty and unnecessary. I disputed the charge with Turo and was refunded after they confirmed the car was returned full. However, the host then called back in trying to dispute my refund, forcing me to contact Turo a second time to resolve the issue all over again. I also tried reaching out to the host directly before escalating things, but received no response.
The unnecessary charge caused an overdraft on my account and made the entire experience stressful, inconvenient, and very unprofessional. I would never rent from this host again.

reviewTitle: I brought the vehicle back and turned…
reviewDescription:

It's not a dent, it's not a scratch.It's a scuff. They are calling this damage. 
Never mind that the vehicle had previously been in an accident and wasn't disclosed. The fender,not fully attached,and hood were obviously not fixed correctly. 
The host, didn't speak English, so no communication occurred when picking up the vehicle.
I had to buy a windshield wiper and windshield wiper fluid just to get the windshield clean enough to see out of. 
The vehicle drove great and we had a great time while having the vehicle. But all this stuff has added up, and now i'm being charged to fix something that can likely be cleaned off the bumper.
I guess we'll see what happens!!

reviewTitle: This was my first time using Turo
reviewDescription:
This was my first time using Turo, and unfortunately it was not a good experience. I went on a trip to Honolulu and needed a rental last minute. I found a vehicle hosted by Aya in Kaneohe, HI. Initially, booking wasn’t an issue. I completed the ID verification and took the required pre-trip photos of the vehicle.

After returning the vehicle, I messaged the host to let her know I had arrived. She read the message but never responded, so I assumed that was all I needed to do. Later on, I received a message regarding the checkout process asking for additional information and post-trip photos after I had already left the location. I was unaware of this requirement, and I believe the checkout protocol should be clearly available and emphasized at the actual return time. Then after I checked out through turo, I’m sure the host got a notification because she finally got back and said to rate her 5 stars, which I did. 

Then, about 24 hours later, the host claimed I caused damage to the vehicle and sent me a photo that appeared edited. I asked for a video of the damage and also asked if I could return to inspect the vehicle myself, but she denied both requests and became defensive. I was simply trying to understand the situation and get clarity. I know exactly how I left the vehicle.

Also, when I picked up the vehicle, it did not look the way it was presented in the listing photos. In the photos, the car appeared very clean, polished, and well maintained on the outside. However, when I arrived, there were already multiple dents and scratches throughout the vehicle. Because of that, if these false damages are now being claimed against me, it’s difficult to believe any money would actually be going toward repairs on a vehicle that already had visible cosmetic issues and clearly needed work done.

To sum it up, be very mindful. I think it’s best going through rentals through the airport.

I am a new Turo user and was not fully familiar with the extension process. My scheduled return time was 12:00 PM. I contacted the host directly before the end of my trip to request a one-hour extension, and she verbally agreed, telling me that Turo was closed on weekends and that's why the app wouldn't allow an extension. Based on her approval, I returned the vehicle at 1:00 PM.
I completed all return steps — I took photos, locked the vehicle, and secured the key. I was unaware that my checkout photos had not uploaded correctly until after I had already left for the airport. I corrected this as soon as I realized the error.
I acted in good faith throughout this process. I had the host's verbal approval for the one-hour extension, returned the car on time per that agreement, and completed the checkout process to the best of my ability as a first-time user. I requested that the charges be waived through 2 phone calls and 2 different emails with attached photos of the text messages between myself and the owner as well as time-stamped photos of the car when I dropped it off to no avail.  I am very frustrated and will probably never use Turo again.

reviewTitle: Turo is a game changer for travel
reviewDescription: Turo is a game changer for travel.  From booking to return the entire process is seamlessly easy.  I love how simple it is to change search parameters on the app to find exactly what you’re looking for in a vehicle.  The rate transparency is a welcomed change to traditional rental car companies with no added or unexpected fees.  Pick up and return could not be any better or more convenient with instructions right from the host as soon as you land at the airport, such a huge time saver.  Real time communication with hosts is also so refreshing.  I’ll never use another company for rental car needs again.

Extremely disappointed with my recent experience with Turo and their claims process.

During my trip, another driver backed into the vehicle, left a note, accepted responsibility, and provided insurance information. I fully cooperated from the beginning and provided everything Turo needed.

What happened next was honestly shocking.

Turo repeatedly told me the third party’s insurance “denied coverage” because the damages were supposedly below the driver’s deductible. Then, almost immediately after emailing me, they charged $500 directly to my credit card before I had any meaningful opportunity to review documents, dispute the determination, or verify the insurance information myself.

Later, the at-fault driver personally contacted Progressive again and was told there may have been a processing error. According to him, Progressive escalated the claim for payment and even stated the deductible should not apply the way Turo described it. The driver provided me with the Progressive representative’s direct contact information and encouraged Turo to contact them directly.

Despite this contradictory information, Turo chose to:

* finalize the matter internally,
* keep the charge on my account,
* and tell ME to personally recover the money from the at-fault driver myself.

That is incredibly unprofessional.

The entire process felt like an abuse of power by a company that knows it can immediately charge customers first and sort things out later. There was no meaningful dispute process, no neutral review, and no real opportunity to resolve conflicting insurance information before funds were removed from my account.

To make matters worse, the actual damage estimate was only around $681 for relatively minor bumper damage. Yet Turo acted as though the matter was completely settled while conflicting information still existed. 

I understand companies have Terms of Service. That is not the issue. The issue is how poorly and aggressively this claim was handled from a customer-service and fairness standpoint.

I expected professionalism and reasonable investigation. Instead, I got automated charges, contradictory explanations, and zero meaningful opportunity to resolve the situation before my card was charged.

Very disappointing experience.

On top of that, I reserved it for 3pm, ended up not picking it up until 3:40 pm after communicating that and returning it at 3:45, he submitted a late fee. I have rented over 40 times with Turo and not EVER a single complaint. I know in the future to never communicate outside of Turo and that Turo clearly doesn’t value loyalty to allow such foolishness.

reviewTitle: No longer using Turo outside the country
reviewDescription: I usually rent through Turo in California. I was traveling through Canada and rented a vehicle as my experience in California has always been great. Typically i always insure the car with the "premium" insurance as that has no out of pocket charge should the vehicle be damaged in any way. When i booked in Canada, there was no premium insurance, only standard. I purchased the standard and unfortunately while driving on the highway, a pebble hit the hood of the car, scratched and dented it through no fault of our own. The damage was minimal that we didn't notice it. The owner noticed it when we returned the vehicle and charged us $100 for repair. Had we had the option for a premier insurance, the owner could have dealt with it on his own with the Turo insurance. Instead i had to have several conversations with the owner via the app while i was still traveling to other destinations with minimal cell phone reception, which caused me a lot of stress while still on my vacation as i felt the host might think i was trying to ignore the conversation. Additionally, there was a disclaimer once the claim was submitted that the host could not charge me more than what my out of pocket amount is. However, i was unable to find on the app what my out of pocket amount is with the standard insurance. Due to all of this, I will no longer use Turo outside of the country again.

Turo charged me an extra $45 for being 4 minutes past the rental period.  I had returned the car and parked it in the designated area just in time.  Yet I was charged this late fee because I still had to take inside and outside pics of the vehicle to prove I had not damaged it.   If you dont do this, you will be liable for any damages that occur to the vehicle between the time you drop it off and the time the vehicle owner returns to it (which could be whenever they feel like it!).  You must check out officially through the Turo app in order to avoid any late charges.  It's not enough to text the vehicle owner that you had returned the vehicle.  In order to officially "check out", you must upload your in and out pics in the Turo app.  That takes time, which is why I was 4 minutes late.  The vehicle owner had no problem with the extra minutes, but Turo has to make their profit.  When I explained the situation to Turo customer service, their response was that I was still 4 minutes late, and "we now consider this case closed."  How's that for customer service?, especially for a new Turo customer like myself.  I, for one, am not impressed. reviewTitle: TURO will take care of you
reviewDescription:
TURO will take care of you! We picked up a tesla at LAX after flying across the country that later started to smell like weed. We didnt notice at first because the owner had a super strong air freshener inside. After we put the freshener in the glove box we immediately started smelling weed. Problem was that it was late and our hotel was an hour away. I contacted TURO from our hotel and told them about the smell and how we wanted to swap the car. They were on our side the whole time. No arguing. 

They offered to book a new vehicle after returning the vehicle (required before they could rebook), but we decided to rebook a car ourselves for the next day so we wouldn't have to wait. It ended up costing an additional 200 over what we originally paid for the original tesla. 

The next day after the drop off of the original car and pickup of the new car from the airport, they refunded the charge for the second car, covering the additional $200. 

Yes, still a bit more hassle than a rental agency but its really the only way to rent a tesla on the west coast at affordable prices. Gas prices are nuts and we drive a lot when visiting. Teslas make sense for us and Turo has been a great rental source for them.

I did arrive a the car roughly 30 minutes for my pickup time. They don't give you check in or drop off procedures until minutes before your scheduled times. reviewTitle: The company needs a feature where you…
reviewDescription:
The company needs a feature where you can record. I’m currently being accused of a false claim by Vince. I rented his fully loaded all power BMW SUV and soon after I picked it up he sends a message about a seat he had to get fixed prior that cost $2000. This is Vince exact text , “We had a guest break a seat while being impatient, and it cost them $2000 to have it repaired.” I knew no one was getting in the backseat while it was in my possession and I knew everything was power! 4 hours after I return he claims a back seat is broken. Vince exact text, “ Hello, one of the middle seats is stuck as it was moved without using the mechanical buttons. We have tried to recalibrate it yet. It is gonna have to be handled by a professional.
I wanted to let you know as we will have to get an estimate for the repair.” I don’t know what seat was broken prior, if it was ever fixed, or if it’s a different back seat.  While the vehicle was in my possession, no one got in the backseat. Only the front driver seat in front passenger seat was sat in. This is the message he sent 4 First and last time I’ll be using Turo. 
Vince exact text
“There was no broken seat when you picked up the car.  We sent you message telling you to use the mechanical buttons as someone previously broke a seat by not using the buttons to move the chair.  That had been repaired long aho.“

I know everything in Vince fully load SUV BMW was power. I have common sense! I didnt get in the backseat and I wouldn’t forcefully try to pull a seat down. Then Vince sent different angle pics that included others scratches/wear and tear to the interior that I never scene because I didn’t get in the backseat.

Our trip isn’t for a few weeks, imagine if booking a trip that’s for tomorrow, and you need a car, you go through the steps, and your reservation is cancelled for review of the account, how helpful is this service then?
Odd.
So now I have to wait for the funds, we’re looking to rent a premium car, so it’s a decent amount, to return to the card - we’re waiting mostly on principle, not due to lack of funds.
I reach out to customer service and they explain it’s because the account is new.
Okay that’s very fair, especially for a car that’s at least 100k.
My question is, if there’s a need to review any/all new accounts shouldn’t that be something that’s said prior to inputting personal and credit card information.
I’m especially annoyed because the trip isn’t for weeks, any review of the account in theory should take place within that time. Not immediately after divulging personal information.
The system is so backwards that the car owner reached out to share pleasantries, and on my end the reservation is cancelled.

This is extremely annoying, tell me the rules and procedures prior to me giving out my information.

Absolutely a horrible, greedy and unethical company. A lot of hosts are corrupt and they will make false claims to make money and offset their bad months. Turo will ALWAYS side with their hosts over the clients, even if the host lies and there's not much of proof. You would just get charged and their customer service will tell you nothing we can do about it. 
Currently, in the process of taking them to court in Canada, along with a few others who were screwed by Turo and I am determined to expose all the scams that some hosts practice and where the platform supports these practices.
I like supporting small business. Renting directly through the owner is personal & more cost effective- win win!

reviewTitle: Great Car Ride. Dubious Host Damage Claim.
reviewDescription:
My Rental Experience:

The host manages at least two identical cars: Jerome Tesla and Versia Tesla, both 2024 Tesla Model 3, black exterior and black interior.
•Rented "Jerome Tesla" for 5 days.
•Returned car early, fully washed, and vacuumed.
•Vehicle ran flawlessly. Paid charging invoices immediately.

The Issue (Suspicious Damage Claim):
•Five hours after return, host submitted a $540.49 damage claim.
•Before/After photos show no discernable difference or damage.
•Host explicitly pressured me to settle cash outside of Turo.

Inconsistent Host Behavior:
•I offered to inspect the car in person in the morning.
•Host claimed the car was to be rented at 1:00 AM at Bellevue Square.
•I waited at the location from midnight to 1:20 AM; the car never appeared.
•Host now claims the car is unavailable for inspection until July 1, 2026.
•Host retaliated with a 1-star review solely because I disputed the claim.

reviewTitle: Picking up and leaving the car were…
reviewDescription: Picking up and leaving the car were easy. John was pleasant and was easy to communicate with. The car is what I expected. I had no issues. I would recommend again.

Car was parked where specified and was the car pictured. 

The tires, specifically the rear passenger tire was completely bald which was extremely unsafe during the rainy conditions. 

I messaged the owner prior to even getting into the vehicle and they informed me there was a tire appointment for the day my rental period started but they did not want to cancel on me. 

I believe drivers safety is more paramount than making a buck. Also, given the tire condition, this should have been changed long before my rental.

Upon exiting the airport, I paid the parking pass which was $59! The cost for a 24 hour period was $24, so clearly the I paid the vehicles “storage fee” while parked multiple days at the airport. When I messaged Turo they said that cannot reimburse me because they do not have an agreement at OGG airport.

When I messaged the owner, she did not respond and left the message read.
